Ayah

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ (۱)
All praise belongs to Allah to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. To Him belongs all praise in the Hereafter, and He is the All-wise, the All-aware. (1)

Key Points

Five Surahs in the Quran begin with the phrase "الحمدللّه" (All praise belongs to Allah). This Ayah expresses the absolute and unconditional ownership (sovereignty) of Allah, "له ما فى السموات" (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens), and the next Ayah expresses the absolute Divine knowledge, "یعلم ما یلج فى الارض" (He knows what penetrates into the earth). So that in the third Ayah, He tells the deniers of the Resurrection (Ma'ad): We are able to establish the Day of Judgment for retribution and reward. Fakhr Razi says: Knowledge connected to action is wisdom (Hikmah), and the one who knows the consequences and secrets of affairs is called Khabeer (All-Aware/All-Knowing).(450)

450) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- Let us learn how to praise Allah from Allah Himself. "الحمد للّه الّذى..." (All praise belongs to Allah, the One who...)

2- Praises must be offered in return for perfection, power, ownership (sovereignty), awareness, and wisdom. "الّذى له ما فى السموات و..." (The One to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and...)

3- In this world, others besides Allah are also praised, but in the Hereafter, praise belongs exclusively to Him. "و له الحمد فى الآخرة" (And to Him belongs the praise in the Hereafter).

4- His praise is not confined to a specific time or special conditions. "و له الحمد فى الآخرة" (And to Him belongs the praise in the Hereafter). The inhabitants of Paradise will say: "و آخر دعواهم أنِ الحمدللّه ربّ العالمین" (And the conclusion of their prayer will be: All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of the worlds).(451)

5- Divine ownership is extensive, wise, and aware. "له ما فى السموات - هو الحكیم الخبیر" (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens - He is the Wise, the All-Aware).

6- The functioning of the cosmic order in this world and the Hereafter is based on Divine wisdom. "له ما فى السموات... و له الحمد فى الآخرة و هو الحكیم" (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens... and to Him belongs the praise in the Hereafter, and He is the Wise). 451) Yunus, 10.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Saba'

This Surah was revealed in Mecca and contains fifty-four Ayahs. It is named "Saba'" due to the account of the people of Saba' mentioned within it. Like other Meccan Surahs, most of its discussions concern matters of belief, especially the Origin (Mabda') and the Resurrection (Ma'ad). Of course, within the story of Prophet Sulayman (Solomon) and his encounter with the people of Saba', there is a reference to some of the Divine blessings and the destiny of the grateful and the disbelievers.

Ayah

يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ (۲)
He knows whatever enters into the earth and whatever emerges from it, and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends into it, and He is the All-merciful, the All-forgiving. (2)

Key Points

After mentioning general principles, stating examples is necessary. Following the word al-Khabīr (the All-Aware), which was general, Allah recounts examples of His knowledge. The instance of «و هو الحكیم الخبیر یعلم ما یلج...» (He is the Wise, the All-Aware. He knows what penetrates...) The instance of «یلج فى الارض» (penetrates the earth) is the entry of the seed into the soil, and the instance of «یخرج منها» (comes forth from it) is the emergence of the plant from the soil, and the instance of «ینزل من السماء» (descends from the heaven) is the descent of rain from the sky, and the instance of «ما یعرج فیها» (what ascends into it) is the flight of birds in the sky. Throughout the entire Quran, the words Ghafūr (Forgiving) and Raḥīm (Merciful) are mentioned together forty-nine times. In all cases, Ghafūr comes first, followed by Raḥīm, except in this Ayah. Perhaps this is because in all other cases, the focus is on the actions of humans, who must first be forgiven and then receive Divine Mercy. But in this Ayah, the primary focus is on Allah's knowledge, grace, and Mercy, and forgiveness related to human actions is implicitly mentioned in the word ya'ruj (ascends). «الرّحیم الغفور» (The Merciful, the Forgiving).

Messages

1- Allah is not only the Owner of existence, but He is also aware of all its details. «یعلم ما یلج...»

2- In the system of creation, things are in motion and circulation (coming and going). «یلج، یخرج، ینزل، یعرج»

3- The sign of His Mercy is the descent of rain from the sky, «ینزل», and the sign of His Forgiveness is the covering of man's ugly deeds. «الرحیم الغفور»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَا تَأْتِينَا ٱلسَّاعَةُ ۖ قُلْ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّى لَتَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ عَـٰلِمِ ٱلْغَيْبِ ۖ لَا يَعْزُبُ عَنْهُ مِثْقَالُ ذَرَّةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَآ أَصْغَرُ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَآ أَكْبَرُ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳)
The faithless say, ‘The Hour will not overtake us.’ Say, ‘Yes, indeed it will surely overtake you, by my Lord,’ the Knower of the Unseen; not [even] an atom’s weight escapes Him in the heavens or in the earth, nor [is there] anything smaller than that nor bigger, but it is in a manifest Book, (3)

Messages

1- Quoting blasphemous statements is permissible for the purpose of criticizing and refuting them. «قال - قل»

2- Safeguard the believers by presenting the views of the opponents and refuting them. «قال - قل»

3- The deniers of the Resurrection merely make a claim and possess no proof for their denial. «لا تأتینا الساعة»

4- The response to blasphemous statements must be firm. «لا تأتینا الساعة... بلى و ربّى...»

5- Allah’s comprehensive knowledge of all things guarantees the realization of the Resurrection. «لتأتینّكم عالم الغیب»

6- The time of the occurrence of the Resurrection is among the matters of the Unseen. «عالم الغیب»

7- The court of the Resurrection is based on the endless Divine knowledge. «عالم الغیب لا یعزب عنه»

8- The size (smallness or largeness) of objects does not affect Allah’s knowledge. «لا اصغر من ذلك و لا اكبر» (Just as seeing a small nail or a tall tower is the same for the eye, and looking at the tower does not exert extra pressure on the eye, similarly, Allah’s knowledge of small and large objects is the same for Him.)

Ayah

لِّيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (٤)
that He may reward those who have faith and do righteous deeds.’ For such there will be forgiveness and a noble provision. (4)

Key Points

"Rizq Karim" (a generous provision) is a provision that is accompanied by abundance, generosity, and honor, not by obligation or pity.

Messages

1- The Resurrection is for receiving rewards. «لیجزى الّذین آمنوا»

2- Only He who is aware of everything can give the true reward and punishment. «عالم الغیب... لیجزى»

3- Allah's mercy precedes His wrath. (In this Ayah, the reward is mentioned, and in the next Ayah, the punishment is discussed.) «لیجزى - لهم عذاب»

4- There is no way to receive a reward except through faith and righteous deeds. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات اولئك لهم»

5- Forgiveness is the prerequisite for receiving blessings. «مغفرة»

6- The provision of the Hereafter is very vast, great, and generous. «رزق كریم»

7- Divine rewards are both spiritual, «مغفرة» (forgiveness), and material. «رزق كریم» (a generous provision).

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ سَعَوْ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا مُعَـٰجِزِينَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مِّن رِّجْزٍ أَلِيمٌ (٥)
But those who contend with Our signs seeking to frustrate [their purpose], for such is a painful punishment due to defilement. (5)

Key Points

Enemies who target sacred things, "Our signs (Ayatuna)," aim to render ineffective and defeat the divine objectives. Allah also punishes them; His punishment is rijz (torment) and a painful chastisement.

Messages

1- Enemies are striving intensely, so you should not be heedless. «سَعَوا فى آیاتنا»

2- Human rebellion reaches the point where one desires to neutralize the divine signs and prevent their effectiveness and impact. «معاجزین»

3- The reward of the righteous believers is from Allah, «لیجزى» (that He may reward), but the punishment of the evildoers is the result of their own actions. «لهم عذاب»

4- Threat and encouragement must be side by side. «لهم مغفرة... لهم عذاب»

Ayah

وَيَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ هُوَ ٱلْحَقَّ وَيَهْدِىٓ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَمِيدِ (٦)
Those who have been given knowledge see that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth and [that] it guides to the path of the All-mighty, the All-laudable. (6)

Messages

1- Knowledge grants insight and vision to humanity. «و یرى الّذین اوتوا العلم»

2- When recounting past history, we should mention the actions of all groups, both believers and disbelievers. «سعوا فى آیاتنا - و یرى الّذین اوتوا العلم»

3- Scholars should consider their knowledge to be from Allah. «اوتوا العلم»

4- The sign of true knowledge is realizing the truthfulness of the Quran and accepting it. Without knowledge and insight, recognizing the truth is impossible. «اوتوا العلم - هو الحقّ»

5- The true scholar accepts what has been revealed from Allah. «الّذى اُنزل الیك»

6- The descent of revelation is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship. «انزل... من ربّك»

7- The standard of truth is revelation and the Quran. «انزل الیك... هو الحقّ»

8- The path of the prophets is the path of honor and dignity. «صراط العزیز الحمید»

9- The Divine path is invincible, and the efforts of the opponents are futile. «سعوا فى آیاتنا معاجزین... صراط العزیز»

10- The powerful ones of the world are usually not beloved, but Allah, the Powerful, is beloved and praised. «العزیز الحمید»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ هَلْ نَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ يُنَبِّئُكُمْ إِذَا مُزِّقْتُمْ كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ إِنَّكُمْ لَفِى خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ (۷)
The faithless say, ‘Shall we show you a man who will inform you [that] when you have been totally rent to pieces you will indeed have a new creation? (7)

Messages

1- The disbelievers propagate against religion and sacred things. «قال الّذین كفروا» But they have no proof except contempt and mockery. «هل ندلّكم»

2- The disbelievers belittle religious figures and sacred things in order to prepare the ground for people to distance themselves (from them). «على رجل»

Ayah

أَفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَم بِهِۦ جِنَّةٌۢ ۗ بَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ وَٱلضَّلَـٰلِ ٱلْبَعِيدِ (۸)
Has he fabricated a lie against Allah, or is there a madness in him?’ Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter languish in punishment and extreme error. (8)

Messages

1- Some disbelievers accept Allah, and even though they do not accept the Resurrection, they consider lying against Allah bad. «افترى على اللّه»

2- Disbelievers follow all paths of deviation, but they do not believe. «افترى - ام به جنّة - لا یؤمنون»

3- Lack of faith in the Hereafter is a type of torment, both in this world and in the Hereafter. (Worry about futility, fear of death, and the waste of efforts is the greatest worldly torment.) «فى العذاب»

4- The person who does not accept the Resurrection has no path to salvation. «الضلال البعید»

Ayah

أَفَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِن نَّشَأْ نَخْسِفْ بِهِمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ أَوْ نُسْقِطْ عَلَيْهِمْ كِسَفًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُّنِيبٍ (۹)
Have they not regarded that which is before them and that which is behind them of the sky and the earth? If We like, We can make the earth swallow them, or let a fragment from the sky fall on them. There is indeed a sign in that for every penitent servant. (9)

Messages

1- For belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and other unseen matters, seek help from observable phenomena. Contemplation on the greatness of the universe is the source of belief in the Resurrection. In the previous Ayah, He said: «لا یؤمنون بالآخرة». In this Ayah, He says: «أفلم یروا الى ما بین...». Why do they not look at the creations?

2- The Hand of Allah is open for any kind of change in the system of existence. «ان نشأ»

3- Alongside logic, threat is also necessary. «أفلم یروا - نخسف بهم الارض»

4- Reflection upon existence is the source of servitude and turning in repentance (inabah) to the threshold of Allah. «أفلم یروا... عبد منیب»

Ayah

۞ وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ مِنَّا فَضْلًا ۖ يَـٰجِبَالُ أَوِّبِى مَعَهُۥ وَٱلطَّيْرَ ۖ وَأَلَنَّا لَهُ ٱلْحَدِيدَ (۱٠)
Certainly We gave David our grace: ‘O mountains and birds, chime in with him!’ And We made iron soft for him, (10)

Key Points

Perhaps the repentance and lamentation of the mountains with David (Dawud) is the same as their glorification (tasbih). «انا سخّرنا الجبال معه یسبّحن بالعشى و الاشراق»(452)

Allah granted twelve virtues to Prophet David عليه السلام: 1. Divine knowledge. «و لقد آتینا داود و سلیمان علما»(453) 2. Prophethood and Messengership. «آتاه اللّه الملك و الحكمة»(454) 3. Heavenly Book. «آتینا داود زبورا»(455) 4. Vicegerency (Khilafah). «یا داود انّا جعلناك خلیفة فى الارض»(456) 5. Stability of rule. «شدّدنا ملكه»(457) 6. Abundant resources. «اوتینا من كل شى ء»(458) 7. Wisdom. «آتیناه الحكمة و فصل الخطاب»(459) 8. Judgment. «فاحكم بین النّاس بالحقّ»(460) 9. The softening of iron in His hand. «اَلَنّا له الحدید» 10. Understanding the speech of birds. «عُلّمنا منطق الطیر»(461) 11. The mountains and birds echoing Him. «یا جبال اوّبى معه و الطّیر» 12. A son like Solomon (Sulayman). «و وهبنا لداود سلیمان»(462)

We read in a Hadith that Allah said to David: "You are a good servant, provided you do not consume from the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal)!" He wept for forty days until Allah softened the iron in his hand, and he began weaving armor. Every day he wove one piece of armor worth a thousand Dirhams, and he wove three hundred and sixty pieces of armor in total.(463)

452) ص، 18. 453) نمل، 15. 454) بقره، 251. 455) اسراء، 55. 456) ص، 26. 457) ص، 20. 458) نمل، 16. 459) ص، 20. 460) ص، 26. 461) نمل، 16. 462) ص، 30. 463) كافى، ج 5، ص 74 و تفسیر نورالثقلین، ج 3، ص 446 به نقل از تفسیر راهنما.

Messages

1- Allah grants special gifts to His chosen servants. «و لقد آتینا داود منّا فضلاً»

2- Existence possesses consciousness and is addressed by Allah. «یا جبال اَوّبى»

3- The supplication (munajat) of creatures is a real matter, not metaphorical. «اوّبى معه»

4- This world and the Hereafter can be combined. (Prophethood and control over existence were combined for David). «النّا له الحدید»

5- The friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) possess cosmic authority (Wilayah Takwini) and the power to influence existence. «النّا له الحدید»

6- Utilizing nature is a virtue. «فضلاً... و النّا له الحدید»

7- Whoever softens his heart for Allah, Allah will also soften iron for him. «اوّبى... النّا له الحدید»

8- Iron smelting has an ancient history. «النّا له الحدید»

9- The friends of Allah were connected to industry. «النّا له الحدید»

Ayah

أَنِ ٱعْمَلْ سَـٰبِغَـٰتٍ وَقَدِّرْ فِى ٱلسَّرْدِ ۖ وَٱعْمَلُوا۟ صَـٰلِحًا ۖ إِنِّى بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۱۱)
saying, ‘Make easy coats of mail, and keep the measure in arranging [the links], and act righteously. Indeed I watch what you do.’ (11)

Key Points

«Sāhighāt» is the plural of sāhigh, meaning a complete and wide coat of mail (armor). The word «sard» means weaving (or braiding/linking). «Wa qaddir fi al-sard» means: be precise in weaving the armor so that it is proportionate and measured.

Messages

1- Industry must be managed by righteous individuals and be for the benefit of the people. «an i‘mal sāhighāt»

2- The products of the men of God must be complete (perfect). «an i‘mal sāhighāt»

3- Industry must be accompanied by precision and quality. «qaddir fi al-sard»

4- Military industries must be for righteous deeds (not for conquest, oppression, or tyranny). «an i‘mal sāhighāt... wa‘malū sālihan»

5- The industry of blacksmithing and the manufacturing of metal tools has a very long history. «an i‘mal sāhighāt...»

6- Faith in Allah's knowledge is the motivation for performing righteous deeds. «wa‘malū sālihan innī bimā ta‘malūna basīr»

Ayah

وَلِسُلَيْمَـٰنَ ٱلرِّيحَ غُدُوُّهَا شَهْرٌ وَرَوَاحُهَا شَهْرٌ ۖ وَأَسَلْنَا لَهُۥ عَيْنَ ٱلْقِطْرِ ۖ وَمِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ مَن يَعْمَلُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِۦ ۖ وَمَن يَزِغْ مِنْهُمْ عَنْ أَمْرِنَا نُذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذَابِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (۱۲)
And for Solomon [We subjected] the wind: its morning course was a month’s journey and its evening course was a month’s journey. We made a fount of [molten] copper flow for him, and [We placed at his service] some of the jinn who would work for him by the permission of his Lord, and if any of them swerved from Our command, We would make him taste the punishment of the Blaze. (12)

Key Points

Ghudūw (غدوّ) means movement towards the morning, and rawāḥ (رواح) means movement from noon onwards. The word ‘ayn (عین) refers to a vessel in which copper is melted. Allah bestowed special favors upon Prophet Sulayman (Solomon), just as He did upon his father, Dawud (David). According to the Quran, the Jinn are accountable, rational, possessing free will, powerful, and industrious beings. In Surah Ṣād, we read: the Jinn performed construction and diving for Sulayman. (464) The subjugation of the wind by Prophet Sulayman meant using it for the movement of clouds, the movement of ships, the pollination of plants, the purification of the air, and other purposes. «و لسلیمان الریح» The (distorted) Torah describes Prophet Sulayman as a tyrannical king, an idol-temple builder, and one who succumbed to the desires of women, which contradicts the Quran. (465)

464) Ṣād, 37. 465) Tafsir Namūneh, quoting the Torah, Book One.

Messages

  1. Prophets possess authority (wilāyah) over the universe and the realm of creation. «و لسلیمان الریح»

  2. Do not rely solely on natural analyses for the subjugation of nature. (The movement of the wind was placed under the control of Allah’s vicegerent by His will.) «و لسلیمان الریح»

  3. Allah’s power is uniform regarding all things. (He subjugated iron for Prophet Dawud and the air for Prophet Sulayman.) «و لسلیمان الریح»

  4. Allah had given Dawud the power to melt iron and Sulayman the power to melt copper. «و اسلنا له عین القطر» (The industry of coppersmithing existed during the time of Prophet Sulayman.)

  5. Humans can subjugate the Jinn. «و من الجنّ مَن یعمل بین یدیه»

  6. The Jinn are assigned to perform tasks for humans by the will and command of Allah. «مَن یعمل بین یدیه باِذن ربّه»

  7. Management requires power and firmness. «و من یزغ منهم عن امرنا... نذقه»

  8. The Jinn possess free will and can disobey the Divine command, «و من یزغ منهم عن امرنا», and in that case, they are punished. «نذقه من عذاب السعیر»

Ayah

يَعْمَلُونَ لَهُۥ مَا يَشَآءُ مِن مَّحَـٰرِيبَ وَتَمَـٰثِيلَ وَجِفَانٍ كَٱلْجَوَابِ وَقُدُورٍ رَّاسِيَـٰتٍ ۚ ٱعْمَلُوٓا۟ ءَالَ دَاوُۥدَ شُكْرًا ۚ وَقَلِيلٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِىَ ٱلشَّكُورُ (۱۳)
They built for him as many temples as he wished, and figures, basins like cisterns, and caldrons fixed [in the ground]. ‘O House of David, act thankfully, and few of My servants are grateful.’ (13)

Key Points

"محاریب" (maḥārīb) is the plural of "محراب" (miḥrāb), meaning either a place of worship or a place of war (ḥarb) and battle, because a special place for defense used to be built on top of palaces. (466) "تماثیل" (tamāthīl) is the plural of timthāl, meaning a statue or sculpture. Perhaps the intention is the making of decorative objects resembling natural things. As stated in a narration from Imam Baqir عليه السلام, the meaning of tamāthīl is the sculpture of trees and plants, not humans or animals. (467) "جِفان" (jifān), plural of jafyah, refers to very large vessels that are like basins. "جَواب" (jawāb), plural of jābiyah, means a basin or cistern. "قدور" (qudūr), plural of qadr, means a pot or cauldron. "راسیات" (rāsiyaāt), plural of rāsiya, means fixed or stationary. Many people offer thanks (shukr) and are thankful (shākir), but shakūr (the constantly thankful) is rare, meaning someone who is perpetually grateful. "قلیل من عبادى الشكور" (Few of My servants are truly grateful).

Solomon and Blessings 1. In response to the blessings, he asked Allah for the success (tawfīq) to be grateful. "ربّ أوزعنى أن اشكر نعمتك" (468) 2. He considered the blessings a means of trial. "هذا من فضل ربّى لیبلونى أأشكر أم أكفر" (469) 3. He was not attracted by the gifts of strangers. "اتمدّونن بمال" (470) 4. He possessed a special scientific understanding. "ففهّمناها سلیمان" (471) 5. He was familiar with the language of the birds. "علّمنا منطق الطیر" (472) 6. He reviewed his armies. "و حُشر لسیلمان جنوده" (473) 7. Birds were at his service. "و تفقّد الطیر" (474) 8. He used all available means for propagating the religion. (Sending a letter via a bird) "اِذهب بكتابى هذا" (475) 9. His officials possessed the ability of tayy al-ardh (folding the earth/instantaneous travel). "قال الّذى عنده علم من الكتاب أنا آتیك به قبل ان یرتدّ الیك طرفك" (476) 10. He had an unparalleled dominion. "هب لى ملكاً لا ینبغى لاحد" (477) 11. He attained a good end and a high station with Allah. "و انّ له عندنا لزلفى و حسن مأب" (478) 12. The jinn were at his service. "و الشیاطین كلّ بنّاء و غوّاص" (479)

466) Tafsir Rahnama. 467) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 468) Al-Ahqaf, 15. 469) An-Naml, 40. 470) An-Naml, 36. 471) Al-Anbiya, 79. 472) An-Naml, 16. 473) An-Naml, 17. 474) An-Naml, 20. 475) An-Naml, 28. 476) An-Naml, 40. 477) Sad, 35. 478) Sad, 25. 479) Sad, 37.

Messages

1- The jinn are artistic and industrial beings. "یعملون له... محاریب و تماثیل" 2- The worker must be under the supervision of the employer. "یعملون له ما یشاء" 3- Pay attention to spiritual matters before material ones. "محاریب" (places of worship) is mentioned before "تماثیل" (statues/decorations). 4- Coppersmithing and casting have a very long history. (The preparation of vessels from molten copper existed during the time of Prophet Solomon.) "جفان" 5- Resources must be a basis for gratitude towards Allah. "اعملوا ال داود شكرا" 6- Gratitude (shukr) is not only verbal but is an action. "اعملوا... شكرا"

Ayah

فَلَمَّا قَضَيْنَا عَلَيْهِ ٱلْمَوْتَ مَا دَلَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَوْتِهِۦٓ إِلَّا دَآبَّةُ ٱلْأَرْضِ تَأْكُلُ مِنسَأَتَهُۥ ۖ فَلَمَّا خَرَّ تَبَيَّنَتِ ٱلْجِنُّ أَن لَّوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ ٱلْغَيْبَ مَا لَبِثُوا۟ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْمُهِينِ (۱٤)
When We decreed death for him, nothing apprised them of his death except a worm which gnawed away at his staff. And when he fell down, [the humans] realized that had the jinn known the Unseen, they would not have remained in a humiliating torment. (14)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: “If anyone could find a ladder to permanence or a way to repel death, it would have been Sulayman (Solomon) son of Dawud (David), for whom the dominion of jinn and mankind was subjugated, along with prophethood and great proximity (to Allah).” (480) If anyone had a path to remain and endure, and could be safe from death, it would surely have been Sulayman, for whom the jinn and mankind were subjugated, and who possessed the station of prophethood and great proximity and status. “Minsa’ah” (منسأة) means a staff, and according to narrations, the intent of “al-‘Adhāb al-Muhīn” (العذاب المهین - the humiliating torment) was the difficult tasks that the jinn were performing. (481)

480) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 182. 481) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The time of death is in Allah’s hand. “Qaḍaynā” (We decreed).

2- Death comes even to those who possess both prophethood and sovereignty simultaneously. “Qaḍaynā ‘alayhi al-mawt” (We decreed death upon him).

3- Animals are Allah’s agents. “Dallahum... dābbat al-arḍ” (showed them... the creature of the earth).

4- The jinn do not know the Unseen. (When Sulayman fell to the ground, the jinn themselves realized they did not know the Unseen, and people also realized that the jinn’s claim of knowing the Unseen was futile.) “Tabayyanat... law kānū ya‘lamūna al-ghayb...” (it became clear... if they had known the Unseen...).

5- The jinn possess feelings of honor and humiliation and seek to escape difficult labor. “Mā labithū fī al-‘adhāb al-muhīn” (they would not have remained in the humiliating torment).

Animals are Allah’s Agents

  1. A piece of a cow’s body revives the murdered person, and he identifies his killer. (In the story of the Cow of the Children of Israel) “Inna Allāha ya’murukum an tadhbaḥū baqarah” (Indeed, Allah commands you to slaughter a cow). (482)

  2. A spider protects the Prophet in the cave. “Illā tanṣurūhu fa-qad naṣarahu Allāh” (If you do not aid him, Allah has already aided him). (483)

  3. A raven becomes a teacher for mankind. “Fa-ba‘atha Allāhu ghurāban” (So Allah sent a raven). (484)

  4. A hoopoe is tasked with delivering Sulayman’s letter to Bilqis. “Idhhab bi-kitābī hādhā” (Go with this letter of mine). (485)

  5. Ababil birds are tasked with crushing the elephant riders. “Wa arsala ‘alayhim ṭayran abābīl” (And He sent against them birds in flocks). (486)

  6. A serpent becomes the means of proving the truthfulness of Moses. “Hiya thu‘bānun mubīn” (It is a clear serpent). (487)

  7. A whale is tasked with punishing Jonah (Yūnus). “Fa-iltaqamahu al-ḥūt” (So the whale swallowed him). (488)

  8. A termite becomes the means of discovering Sulayman’s death. “Ta’kulu minsa’atah” (eating his staff). (489)

  9. The dog of the Companions of the Cave is tasked with guarding them. “Wa kalbuhum bāsiṭun dhirā‘ayhi bi-al-waṣīd” (And their dog stretching its forelegs at the entrance). (490)

  10. Four birds become the cause of Abraham’s (Ibrāhīm’s) reassurance. “Fa-khudh arba‘ata min al-ṭayr” (So take four birds). (491)

  11. A donkey becomes the cause of Uzayr’s certainty in the Resurrection. “Wa unẓur ilā ḥimārik” (And look at your donkey). (492)

  12. Camels, cows, and sheep become Divine symbols (Sha‘ā’ir) during Hajj. “Wa al-budna ja‘alnāhā lakum min sha‘ā’ir Allāh” (And the camels—We have appointed them for you as among the symbols of Allah). (493)

  13. An animal becomes a means of knowing God. “Afalā yanẓurūna ilā al-ibili kayfa khuliqat” (Do they not look at the camels—how they were created?). (494)

  14. An animal becomes a means of testing mankind. “Tanāluhu aydīkum wa rimāḥukum” (which your hands and your spears can reach). (495)

  15. An animal becomes a Divine miracle. “Hādhihi nāqat Allāh” (This is the she-camel of Allah). (496)

  16. An animal becomes a means of Divine wrath. “Al-jarādu wa al-qamalu wa al-ḍafādi‘u” (the locusts, and the lice, and the frogs). (497)

Several Surahs in the Quran are named after animals: Al-Baqarah (The Cow), Al-An‘ām (The Cattle), An-Naḥl (The Bee), An-Naml (The Ant), Al-‘Ankabūt (The Spider), and Al-Fīl (The Elephant).

Ayah

لَقَدْ كَانَ لِسَبَإٍ فِى مَسْكَنِهِمْ ءَايَةٌ ۖ جَنَّتَانِ عَن يَمِينٍ وَشِمَالٍ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِ رَبِّكُمْ وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ لَهُۥ ۚ بَلْدَةٌ طَيِّبَةٌ وَرَبٌّ غَفُورٌ (۱٥)
There was certainly a sign for Sheba in their habitation: two gardens, to the right and to the left. ‘Eat of the provision of your Lord and give Him thanks: a good land and an all-forgiving Lord!’ (15)

Key Points

"Saba'" is the name of a fertile land in Yemen. Saba' was the name of a great personality in the region, after whom the land was named. People have two types of reactions when achieving civilization and benefiting from divine blessings: Some, like Sulayman (Solomon) عليه السلام, despite possessing immense power, ask Allah for the grace to be thankful: "اوزعنى ان اشكر" (inspire me to be grateful). Others, like the people of Saba', committed ingratitude (kufrān) despite having a pure and pleasant city. Wherever the command to eat appears in the Quran, a corresponding responsibility is mentioned alongside it: "كلوا... واشكروا" (Eat... and be grateful) (498) "فكلوا... اطعموا" (So eat... [and] feed) (499) "كلوا... واعملوا صالحا" (Eat... and do righteous deeds) (500) "كلوا... لا تسرفوا" (Eat... [but] do not be extravagant) (501)

498) Baqarah, 172. 499) Hajj, 28. 500) Mu'minun, 51. 501) A'raf, 31.

Messages

1- The history of the land of Saba' is a source of lesson. "لقد كان لسبأ... آیة"

2- The greenery of the gardens in the Saba' region was exceptional. "آیة"

3- Natural beauty is one of the signs (Ayat) of Allah. "آیة جنّتان..."

4- The region of Saba' was pure from all kinds of shortages, calamities, insecurity, immorality (fahshā), and famine. "بلدة طیّبة"

5- One must be grateful for divine blessings, but man never fully renders the right of gratitude, and Allah forgives him. "واشكروا له... ربّ غفور"

6- When choosing a residence, pay attention to the elements of prosperity, purity, and cleanliness of the city. "مسكنهم... بلدة طیّبة"

7- Providing sustenance (Rizq) and granting forgiveness are among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). "رزق ربّكم... ربّ غفور"

Ayah

فَأَعْرَضُوا۟ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَيْلَ ٱلْعَرِمِ وَبَدَّلْنَـٰهُم بِجَنَّتَيْهِمْ جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَوَاتَىْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍ وَأَثْلٍ وَشَىْءٍ مِّن سِدْرٍ قَلِيلٍ (۱٦)
But they disregarded [the path of Allah], so We unleashed upon them a violent flood and replaced their two gardens with two gardens bearing bitter fruit, tamarisk, and sparse lote trees. (16)

Key Points

The word 'Arim (عَرِم) either means violence/severity, and Sayl al-'Arim (سیل العَرِم) means a devastating flood, or it means a dam, meaning a flood that resulted from the breaking of the dam. Ukul (اُكُل) means ma'kul (that which is eaten) and foodstuff. Khamt (خَمط) refers to a bitter plant, and Athl (اَثل) is the tamarisk tree and thorny tree. The Quran repeatedly speaks about the turning away (i'rad) of people: Sometimes turning away from the remembrance of Allah. «و مَن أعرض عن ذكرى»(502) Sometimes turning away from the signs (Ayat) of Allah. «و اِن یَروا آیة یعرضوا»(503) Sometimes turning away from the blessings of Allah. (This very Ayah) and the Ayah «اذا أنعمنا على الانسان أعرض»(504) And sometimes turning away from the Prophet of Allah. «هو نبأ عظیم . أنتم عنه معرضون»(505)

502) Taha, 124. 503) Al-Qamar, 2. 504) Al-Isra, 83. 505) Sad, 67 - 68.

Messages

1- Turning away and ingratitude lead to divine wrath. «فاعرضوا فارسلنا»

Gratitude for blessings increases your blessings Ingratitude takes the blessing out of your hand.

2- Floods, earthquakes, storms, and thunderbolts are not accidental. «ارسلنا... سیل العرم»

3- Nature is commissioned to execute divine wrath. «سیل العرم»

4- Blessings are sometimes increased, «فى كلّ سنبلة مأة حبّه»(506) sometimes decreased, «نقص من الاموال و الانفس و الثمرات»(507) and sometimes transformed. «بدّلناهم...»

506) Al-Baqarah, 261. 507) Ibid., 155.

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ جَزَيْنَـٰهُم بِمَا كَفَرُوا۟ ۖ وَهَلْ نُجَـٰزِىٓ إِلَّا ٱلْكَفُورَ (۱۷)
We requited them with that for their ingratitude. Do We not requite ingrates? (17)

Messages

1- The source of misfortunes is our own negative performance. «ذلك جزیناهم بما بما كفروا»

2- Divine retributions are a tradition and law for the wicked and are not exclusive to the people of Saba'. «هل نجازى الاّ الكفور»

3- That which brings about Divine wrath is insistence upon disbelief (kufr) and its continuation. «هل نجازى الاّ الكفور»

Ayahs

وَجَعَلْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ ٱلْقُرَى ٱلَّتِى بَـٰرَكْنَا فِيهَا قُرًى ظَـٰهِرَةً وَقَدَّرْنَا فِيهَا ٱلسَّيْرَ ۖ سِيرُوا۟ فِيهَا لَيَالِىَ وَأَيَّامًا ءَامِنِينَ (۱۸)
We had placed between them and the towns which We had blessed hamlets prominent [from the main route], and We had ordained the course through them: ‘Travel through them in safety, night and day.’ (18)
فَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا بَـٰعِدْ بَيْنَ أَسْفَارِنَا وَظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ أَحَادِيثَ وَمَزَّقْنَـٰهُمْ كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ (۱۹)
But they said, ‘Our Lord! Make the stages between our journeys far apart,’ and they wronged themselves. So We turned them into folktales and caused them to disintegrate totally. There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [servant]. (19)

Key Points

Aḥādīth (احادیث) is the plural of uḥdūthah (اُحدوثه), meaning a story or piece of news that people relate to one another, either for entertainment or as an example.

Allah had bestowed several special blessings upon the people of Saba: A: Blessed and fertile regions. «باركنا فیها» B: Residential areas and homes close together and visible. «قُرى ظاهرةً» C: Ease of travel, such that movement was possible day and night. «سیروا فیها لیالى و ایّاما» D: The blessing of security. «آمنین»

The Story of the People of Saba As understood from these verses and some narrations, the people of Saba were a community living in the south of the Arabian Peninsula and possessed a high and brilliant civilization. This region lacked a major river, and rainwater was wasted in the plains. Therefore, the people decided to build a large dam and store the water behind it. At the end of one of the valleys, near the city of Ma'rib, they built a massive earthen dam, which became famous by that name. The water released from this dam caused the flourishing of both sides of the river that flowed from the base of the dam, and many villages with beautiful orchards and farmlands appeared on both banks. The distance between these villages was very short, almost continuous, and the abundance of blessings coupled with security had prepared a prosperous environment for life. However, they forgot Allah, engaged in boasting, and fueled class differences. The wealthy did not want low-income individuals to travel between these regions like them or alongside them; they wanted this privilege to be exclusive to themselves.

As recorded in history, field mice attacked the wall of this earthen dam and weakened it from within, such that when a massive flood occurred, the dam wall broke, and a huge torrent flowed, burying the great civilization of Saba under mud and silt. A flood that, according to the Quran, left nothing of that prosperous land except the bitter Arak trees, the salty Ghaz (tamarisk) trees, and a few Sidr (lote) trees.(508) «فارسلنا علیهم سیل العرم و بدّلناهم بجنّتیهم جنّتین ذواتى اُكلٍ خمط و أثل و شى ء من سدر قلیل»

508) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The people of Saba believed in Allah and used the word Rabbanā (Our Lord) in their prayer. «ربّنا باعد»

2- Ingratitude is oppression against oneself. «باعد بین اسفارنا و ظلموا انفسهم»

3- The disintegration and scattering of the people of Saba became the subject of gatherings and a proverb among people. «فجعلناهم احادیث»

4- Ingratitude leads to the collapse of human life. «كلّ ممزّق»

5- Individuals and nations can learn lessons from history and remain steadfast only if they are very patient and grateful. «لآیات لكلّ صبّار شكور»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ صَدَّقَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِبْلِيسُ ظَنَّهُۥ فَٱتَّبَعُوهُ إِلَّا فَرِيقًا مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲٠)
Certainly Iblis had his conjecture come true about them. So they followed him—all except a part of the faithful. (20)

Messages

1- Ingratitude for blessings is the path of penetration and success for Iblis. «ربّنا باعد بین اسفارنا... و لقد صدق علیهم ابلیس ظنه»

2- Iblis does not have certainty regarding his success in leading people astray. «ظنّه»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ لَهُۥ عَلَيْهِم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍ إِلَّا لِنَعْلَمَ مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِمَّنْ هُوَ مِنْهَا فِى شَكٍّ ۗ وَرَبُّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ حَفِيظٌ (۲۱)
He had no authority over them, but that We may ascertain those who believe in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt about it, and your Lord is watchful over all things. (21)

Key Points

Considering that Allah is All-Knowing, the Preserver, the Watcher, and the Witness over everything, the intent of the phrase «لنعلم...» (that We may know...) is to distinguish and specify. That is, to make it clear to humanity and others who is the believer (mu'min) and who is the disbeliever (kafir), or to separate the followers of Iblis from the believers.

Messages

1- Satan cannot compel humans, and people's following of Iblis is based on their own free will and decision. «و ما كان له علیهم من سلطان»

2- Belief in the Day of Judgment is a barrier against Satan. «لنعلم من یؤمن بالآخرة»

3- The philosophy behind the existence of Satan and his temptations is to create multiple paths before humanity, granting them the power of choice and testing them. «الاّ لنعلم...»

4- By harboring doubt and instability, man opens the way for Satan's dominance over himself. «ما كان له علیهم من سلطان... ممن هو منها فى شك»

5- Man has free will in choosing his path and actions. «من یؤمن بالآخرة ممن هو منها فى شك»

6- Forgetfulness and error have no place in Allah's knowledge. «حفیظ»

Ayah

قُلِ ٱدْعُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا لَهُمْ فِيهِمَا مِن شِرْكٍ وَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْهُم مِّن ظَهِيرٍ (۲۲)
Say, ‘Invoke those whom you claim [to be gods] besides Allah! They do not control [even] an atom’s weight in the heavens or the earth, nor do they have any share in [either of] them, nor is any of them His helper.’ (22)

Messages

1- Anything besides Allah is merely an illusion. «زعمتم من دون اللّه»

2- The polytheist lacks sound reasoning (or logic). «زعمتم»

3- Those besides Allah are neither owners of the heavens and the earth, nor partners nor helpers of Him. «لا یملكون - من شرك - من ظهیر»

Ayah

وَلَا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا لِمَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُۥ ۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فُزِّعَ عَن قُلُوبِهِمْ قَالُوا۟ مَاذَا قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (۲۳)
Intercession is of no avail with Him, except for those whom He permits. When fear is lifted from their hearts, they say, ‘What did your Lord say?’ They say, ‘The truth, and He is the All-exalted, the All-great.’ (23)

Key Points

The word "fuzzi'a" (فُزّع), whenever accompanied by the particle "an" (عن), means the removal of wailing (509), but if it is without it, it means causing one to wail. The phrase "الاّ لمن اذن له" (except for him whom He permits) can be interpreted in two ways: A: Intercession before Allah is of no benefit, except for the intercession of the Prophets and the Awliya (saints/close servants) and those intercessors who have permission for intercession from Allah. B: Intercession before Allah is of no benefit, except for those sinners for whom Allah grants permission for intercession to the Awliya. In the Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan, the Ayah under discussion is interpreted as follows: On the Day of Judgment, intercession is of no benefit except for those individuals whom Allah permits. The criminals on that Day will be terrified and wailing until their voices are silenced and the wailing and terror (faza') depart from their hearts. The angels or the believers will ask these criminals: What did your Lord say to you in the world? They will say: He spoke, but we did not listen.

509) Mufradat Raghib.

Messages

1- Imaginary deities do not have permission to intercede for their followers. «لا تنفع الشفاعة»

2- Intercession does not mean that the intercessors are more merciful than Allah, because it is Allah Who grants permission for intercession to the intercessors. «لمن اذن له»

3- Intercession is not an independent act against His definitive Will, but rather it is in line with the Will of Allah. «اذن له»

4- Intercession is possible even on the Day of Judgment, but only with Divine permission. «لا تنفع الشفاعة عنده الاّ لمن أذن له»

5- The permission that Allah grants to individuals to intercede or to be interceded for is based on truth (Haqq). That is, the intercessors reach that status due to their proximity to Allah, and those who are interceded for become worthy of receiving intercession due to maintaining their connection with the path of Allah. «قالوا ماذا قال ربّكم قالوا الحقّ»

Ayah

۞ قُلْ مَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَإِنَّآ أَوْ إِيَّاكُمْ لَعَلَىٰ هُدًى أَوْ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲٤)
Say, ‘Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?’ Say, ‘Allah! Indeed either we or you are rightly guided or in manifest error.’ (24)

Messages

1- In education and training, we should awaken the conscience of the audience and engage their minds by using the method of questioning. «قل مَن یرزقكم...»

2- One of the factors leading to worship is paying attention to the Provider (of sustenance). Think: Who provides sustenance for you? «مَن یرزقكم»

3- The sustenance from the heavens is light, heat, rain, wind, and clouds; and the sustenance from the earth includes all the fruits and blessings of the earth. «یرزقكم من السموات و الارض»

4- All the heavens are effective in providing our sustenance (Rizq). «یرزقكم من السموات»

5- In discussion, dialogue, and good argumentation with others, we must observe fairness and deal gently with them. «انّا او ایّاكم»

6- The guided ones, like those positioned atop a mount or a high vehicle, possess a broad view and insight, «على هُدىً» while the misguided are like those trapped at the bottom of a valley or in the depths of the sea, surrounded by factors of deviation. «فى ضلال مبین»

Ayahs

قُل لَّا تُسْـَٔلُونَ عَمَّآ أَجْرَمْنَا وَلَا نُسْـَٔلُ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۲٥)
Say, ‘You will not be questioned about our guilt, nor shall we be questioned about what you do.’ (25)
قُلْ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنَا رَبُّنَا ثُمَّ يَفْتَحُ بَيْنَنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَهُوَ ٱلْفَتَّاحُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۲٦)
Say, ‘Our Lord will bring us together, then He will judge between us with justice, and He is the All-knowing Judge.’ (26)

Key Points

The word fath (فتح) means removing complexity, whether it is apparent, such as opening a lock, or internal, such as ending a dispute.(510) Al-Fattah (فتّاح) is one of the beautiful Names of Allah.(511)

510) Tafsir-e Nemuneh. 511) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The insistence of the Prophets on guiding people stems from compassion, not because the compensation for people's sins is incumbent upon the Prophets, or vice versa. «قل لا تسئلون عما اجرمنا»

2- Humility and etiquette are means to success. (He refers to His own action as a "sin/crime" (jirm) and the actions of others as a "deed" (amal).) «اجرمنا - تعملون»

3- If your guidance is not effective in this world, do not worry, because everyone will be accounted for on the Day of Judgment. «قل یجمع بیننا ربّنا»

4- The Day of Judgment is both Yawm al-Jam' (the Day of Gathering of all people) «یجمع بیننا» and Yawm al-Fasl (the Day of Separation of the wicked from the righteous). «ثم یفتح»

5- Judgment on the Day of Resurrection is based on truth/justice. «یفتح بیننا بالحقّ»

6- Attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) safeguards a person's stances and actions. «یفتح بیننا بالحقّ» (The file of all tricks, hypocrisies, conspiracies, political and social complexities, and so on, will one day be opened and accounted for.)

7- No one but Allah has the power to resolve complexities, nor is anyone aware of all of them. «هو الفتاح العلیم»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرُونِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَلْحَقْتُم بِهِۦ شُرَكَآءَ ۖ كَلَّا ۚ بَلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۲۷)
Say, ‘Show me those whom you associate with Him as partners.’ No! [They can never show any such partner]. Indeed, He is Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (27)

Key Points

In this Ayah and the preceding Ayahs, the method of gradual, step-by-step, and gentle dialogue with opponents is explained, including:

Firstly: In Ayah 24, He invites them to reflect. "Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?" (من یرزقكم من السموات والارض). So, think.

Secondly: We should deal justly and say: "Either we or you are rightly guided" (انّا او ایاكم). In a discussion, we should not immediately and definitively condemn our opponent.

Thirdly: In Ayah 25, He states: Everyone is responsible for his own thoughts and actions. Our insistence must stem from compassion.

Fourthly: In Ayah 26, He states: In discussions, we should refer to the Day of Resurrection, which is the day the truth will become manifest. (یفتح بیننا بالحقّ...)

Fifthly: In debate and dialogue, we should ask the opponent for proof and evidence. "Show Me the idols that you consider His partners, what have they done?" (This very Ayah).

Messages

1- A question accompanied by reproach is a means of awakening dormant consciences. (قل أرونى)

2- None of the worshipped entities (whether Jesus عليه السلام, the angels, or the idols) consider themselves partners of Allah; it is you who attach them to Him as partners. (الحقتم به شركاء)

3- The Might (Azzah) and invulnerability of Allah prevent any attachment of imaginary and fabricated deities to Him. (و هو العزیز الحكیم)

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ إِلَّا كَآفَّةً لِّلنَّاسِ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۲۸)
We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and warner to all mankind, but most people do not know. (28)

Key Points

The word "كافّة" (kāffah) either means "all" (jamee'), meaning the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is a prophet for all people—a meaning confirmed by other verses and narrations—or it means "restrainer," meaning the Prophet restrains people from disbelief, polytheism, and sin, and the letter tā in the word "كافّة" is for emphasis. Of these two meanings, the first is better. (512)

512) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The mission (risālah) of the Prophet of Islam is universal. «ارسلناك... كافّةً للناس»

2- The most important role of the prophets in guiding people is warning and giving glad tidings. «ما ارسلناك الاّ... بشیراً و نذیراً»

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۲۹)
They say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’ (29)
قُل لَّكُم مِّيعَادُ يَوْمٍ لَّا تَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ عَنْهُ سَاعَةً وَلَا تَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (۳٠)
Say, ‘Your promised hour is a day that you shall neither defer nor advance by an hour.’ (30)

Key Points

In numerous verses, we read: No one knows the time of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) except Allah, and this is part of Divine wisdom so that man may always remain in a state of readiness. Furthermore, not knowing the details does not prevent belief in the generalities. For example: You know from the sound of the doorbell that someone is behind the door, even though you do not know the details of that person—whether they are a woman or a man, what their age or education level is. Not knowing these specifics does not harm your knowledge and belief that someone rang the doorbell. Yes, we have knowledge of the Resurrection, even if we do not know its time. In Ayah 15 of Surah Ta Ha, we read: «انّ الساعة آتیة أكاد اُخفیها» (Indeed, the Hour is coming; I almost conceal it.) The Resurrection is certainly coming, but I have concealed Its time from you.

Messages

1- It is not necessary to know the answer to every question. They asked when the Resurrection would be? «متى هذا الوعد» (When is this promise?) Say: I do not know its time. «میعاد یوم» (The appointed time of a Day.)

2- The Resurrection is a very important day. (The indefinite word «یومٍ» accompanied by tanwin [nunation] is a sign of its grandeur and greatness.)

3- The time of the Resurrection's occurrence is unchangeable. «لا تستأخرون ساعة و لا تستقدمون» (You cannot delay it by an hour, nor advance it.)

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَلَا بِٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ۗ وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ مَوْقُوفُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ يَرْجِعُ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ٱلْقَوْلَ يَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أَنتُمْ لَكُنَّا مُؤْمِنِينَ (۳۱)
The faithless say, ‘We will never believe in this Quran, nor in what was [revealed] before it.’ But if you were to see when the wrongdoers will be made to stop before their Lord casting the blame on one another. Those who were abased will say to those who were arrogant, ‘Had it not been for you, we would surely have been faithful.’ (31)

Messages

1- The disbelievers, due to obstinacy, are never willing to believe. «و قال الّذین كفروا لن نؤمن»

2- The disbelievers, in reality, did not accept any of the heavenly Books. «لن نؤمن بهذا القرآن و لا بالّذى بین یدیه»

3- Belief in the heavenly Books preceding the Quran was also enjoined by the Prophet of Islam, and the disbelievers ignored this injunction. «و لا بالّذى بین یدیه»

4- The court of the Day of Judgment is extremely severe. «و لو ترى»

5- The polytheists oppress themselves. «اذ الظالمون»

6- The polytheists will have to deal with Allah. «موقوفون عند ربّهم»

7- Depicting the severe scenes of the Day of Judgment is the best means of moral training. «موقوفون عند ربّهم»

8- The oppressed (mustad'afūn) who followed the arrogant (mustakbirūn) will also be held accountable. «یقول الّذین استضعفوا للّذین استكبروا»

9- The scene of the Day of Judgment is a scene of dialogue and dispute. «یقول الّذین...»

10- On the Day of Judgment, the criminals, seeking to justify their sin, will say to their leaders: If it were not for you, we would not have committed all these sins. «لولا انتم»

11- On the Day of Judgment, faith is the factor of salvation. «لكنا مؤمنین»

Ayah

قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوٓا۟ أَنَحْنُ صَدَدْنَـٰكُمْ عَنِ ٱلْهُدَىٰ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَآءَكُم ۖ بَلْ كُنتُم مُّجْرِمِينَ (۳۲)
Those who were arrogant will say to those who were abased, ‘Did we keep you from guidance after it had come to you? No, you were guilty [yourselves].’ (32)

Messages

1- A corrupt society and arrogant individuals do not compel a person toward deviation. «انحن صددناكم... بل كنتم مجرمین»

2- On the Day of Resurrection, even the arrogant will understand that the path of the prophets was the truth. «صددناكم عن الهدى»

3- The truth reaches everyone, and each person must accept it himself. «جاءكم»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, one cannot shift the blame for his sins onto others. «بل كنتم مجرمین»

5- The greatest disgrace is that the major criminals label the subordinate people as criminals. «بل كنتم مجرمین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ بَلْ مَكْرُ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ إِذْ تَأْمُرُونَنَآ أَن نَّكْفُرَ بِٱللَّهِ وَنَجْعَلَ لَهُۥٓ أَندَادًا ۚ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلْأَغْلَـٰلَ فِىٓ أَعْنَاقِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۳۳)
Those who were abased will say to those who were arrogant, ‘No, [it was your] night and day plotting, when you prompted us to forswear Allah and to set up equals to Him.’ They will hide their remorse when they sight the punishment, and We will put iron collars around the necks of the faithless. Shall they not be requited for what they used to do? (33)

Key Points

In this Ayah, we read that the criminals conceal their regret, while in other verses and narrations, it is stated that they disclose their regret. This difference is because the stations (or stages) of the Day of Judgment are varied. Although, according to Raghib in his Mufradat, "اسرّوا النّدامة" (asarrū an-nadāmah) means both the disclosure of regret and the concealment of regret, based on the hadith narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, the intended meaning is the concealment of regret. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in response to the question of why they keep their regret hidden in their hearts, said: It is so that they may remain safe from the reproach of others.(513)

513) Bihar, Vol. 7, p. 188.

Messages

1- The arrogant ones strive day and night. «بل مكر الیل و النهار»

2- The leaders of disbelief command based on deceit and planning. «مكر الیل... اذ تأمروننا»

3- The intellectual and cultural deviation of society is the result of the continuous work of the arrogant enemies. «مكر الیل و النهار اذ تأمروننا»

4- The interaction of the arrogant with their subordinates is one of command and prohibition, not invitation and guidance. «تأمروننا أن نكفر»

5- Regret on the Day of Judgment is useless. «اسروا الندامة... جعلنا الاغلال»

6- Divine punishments are commensurate with the actions of the disbelievers. «هل یجزون الا ما كانوا یعملون»

Ayahs

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِى قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّذِيرٍ إِلَّا قَالَ مُتْرَفُوهَآ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۳٤)
We did not send a warner to any town without its affluent ones saying, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.’ (34)
وَقَالُوا۟ نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوَٰلًا وَأَوْلَـٰدًا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ (۳٥)
They say, ‘We have greater wealth and more children, and we will not be punished!’ (35)

Messages

1- Opposition to the Prophet of Islam is not new; they opposed other prophets as well. (Allah consoles His Prophet.) «و ما ارسلنا فى قریة من نذیر الاّ قال مترفوها»

2- The heedless affluent have always been in the front ranks of the enemies of the prophets. «قال مترفوها»

3- Seeking luxury makes a person indifferent and hard-hearted, leading to arrogance. «قال مترفوها... كافرون»

4- Opposition to the prophets is opposition to their message, not to their person. «بما ارسلتم به كافرون»

5- The movement of the prophets endangers the interests of the capitalists. «مترفوها... كافرون»

6- The disbelievers imagined that worldly sufficiency guaranteed immunity in the Hereafter. «اكثر اموالاً... و ما نحن بمعذبین»

7- The wealthy boast about their wealth and children. «نحن اكثر اموالاً و اولاداً»

8- The rich consider themselves a separate, unique class. «ما نحن بمعذبین»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّى يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳٦)
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes and He tightens it, but most people do not know.’ (36)

Messages

1- The Prophet must correct the people's deviant beliefs so that they do not assume that Allah's blessings are always a sign of His favor and grace. «قل انّ ربّى...»

2- The giving and taking away of blessings is not a sign of divine favor or wrath. (He might take from a believer and give to a disbeliever.) «انّ ربّى یبسط الرزق»

3- The giving and taking of sustenance (rizq) is among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «اّن ربّى یبسط»

4- People consider the cause of sustenance to be intermediaries, planning, and their own work, and they are heedless of Allah. «و لكن اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»

5- In a religious society, the view of economics must also have a spiritual and divine aspect. «قالوا نحن اكثر اموالاً... قل ان ربّى یبسط»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُكُم بِٱلَّتِى تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِندَنَا زُلْفَىٰٓ إِلَّا مَنْ ءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزَآءُ ٱلضِّعْفِ بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَهُمْ فِى ٱلْغُرُفَـٰتِ ءَامِنُونَ (۳۷)
It is not your wealth, nor your children, that will bring you close to Us in nearness, excepting those who have faith and act righteously. It is they for whom there will be a twofold reward for what they did, and they will be secure in lofty abodes. (37)

Key Points

Allah has made faith, righteous deeds, spending (in charity/infaq), and sincerity the means of drawing near to Him, but some groups consider wealth, children, and lineage to be the factors of proximity and felicity. In Ayah 15 of Surah At-Taghabun, we read: «انّما اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة و اللّه عنده اجر عظیم» (Indeed, your wealth and your children are only a trial, and Allah—with Him is a great reward.) Indeed, wealth and children are a means of testing you, and it is Allah with Whom is the great reward. Of course, the mention of wealth and children is by way of example; otherwise, anything other than faith and righteous deeds will not be a means of proximity (to Allah). Those who boast about abundant wealth and children should know that the abundance of wealth and children is not always a sign of expanded provision (Rizq); rather, sometimes Allah punishes individuals through their capital. «فلا تعجبك اموالهم و لا اولادهم انّما یرید اللّه لیعذّبهم بها»(514)

514) Tawbah, 55.

Messages

1- Respond to the false propaganda and thoughts of the enemies. The disbelievers (in Ayah 35) used to say: We will not be punished because we have more wealth and children. «نحن اكثر اموالاً و اولاداً و ما نحن بمعذبین» (We are greater in wealth and children, and we are not to be punished.) This Ayah responds: wealth and children are not a cause for remaining safe from punishment. «و ما اموالكم و لا اولادكم...»

2- Proximity to Allah has always been a goal for humans, but sometimes humans have chosen incorrect paths and means to reach it, such as idol worship. «ما نعبدهم الاّ لیقرّبونا الى اللّه زلفى »(515) (We only worship them so that they may bring us nearer to Allah in proximity.) «و ما اموالكم... عندنا زلفى »

3- Possessing wealth, children, and other resources is not a sign of growth and perfection; rather, the correct utilization of them is perfection. «و ما اموالكم... الاّ مَن آمن و عمل صالحاً» (Yes, if wealth and children are aligned with faith and righteous deeds, they are a means of proximity.) «و ما اموالكم... الاّ من امن و عمل»

4- Although the reward is given double, it is still in return for the deed. «جزاء الضعف بما عملوا»

5- When choosing a dwelling, consider other features besides security: having chambers (Ghuraft) and upper floors, a better view, more light, and fresher air. «فى الغرفات آمنون»

6- In Paradise, there is no sickness, fear, sorrow, or anxiety. «آمنون»

515) Az-Zumar, 3.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَسْعَوْنَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا مُعَـٰجِزِينَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ (۳۸)
As for those who contend with Our signs seeking to frustrate [their purpose], they will be brought to the punishment. (38)

Messages

1- Wealthy individuals who constantly use their fortunes to overthrow the truth and make it appear weak will be punished. (Considering the previous Ayah and this Ayah). «یسعون فى آیاتنا»

2- The enemy constantly strives to destroy the truth. «یسعون فى آیاتنا»

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّى يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُۥ ۚ وَمَآ أَنفَقْتُم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُۥ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (۳۹)
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever of His servants that He wishes and tightens it, and He will repay whatever you may spend, and He is the best of providers.’ (39)

Key Points

A person asked Imam Sadiq علیه السلام: If Allah replaces what has been spent (in charity), then why is it that whatever I spend, its replacement does not reach me?! The Imam replied: "If the wealth is lawful (halal) and is spent for a lawful purpose, it will certainly be compensated."(516) Furthermore, its compensation might be in the Hereafter, or through the repulsion of calamity, or by its replacement reaching the next generation in this world, or it might be compensated through means other than wealth.

The word "یقدر" (yaqdir) derived from qadr means both measurement/estimation and restriction/tightening.(517) But here, based on the context provided by the word "یبسط" (yabsut - expands), it means restriction/tightening.

The issue of the expansion and restriction of provision was also raised in Ayah 36, but the addressees there were the disbelievers, while the addressees of this Ayah are the believers.

A merchant who sees his capital exposed to ruin is willing to sell his goods cheaply or on credit, and if a good customer appears and he refuses to sell, he is foolish. Our life is also capital exposed to ruin, and Allah buys it at a high price; refusing to trade with Him is foolishness.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: "Every night a caller announces: «هب للمنفق خلفاً و هب للمسك تلفا» (O Allah!) Grant compensation and reward to the spender, and destroy the wealth of the stingy and the miser."(518)

Allah is the Best Provider. «خیر الرّازقین» (Khayr al-Rāziqīn) because: 1. Everything is in His hands, and He is able to bestow. 2. He is not stingy. 3. He bestows upon everyone. 4. His bestowal is without obligation (minnah) and without expectation. 5. It is permanent. 6. He knows the needs. 7. He bestows things that others are incapable of bestowing.

516) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 486. 517) Mufradat Raghib. 518) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The difference in provision is in His hands and is a means for the training and perfection of man. (The requirement of Allah's Lordship is the restriction and expansion of provisions.) «ربّى یبسط... و یقدر»

2- Spending (in charity) is important, not its amount. «ما انفقتم من شى ء»

3- Spending is not limited to wealth; rather, one can spend from every blessing that Allah has granted to man. «ما انفقتم من شى ء»

4- Certainty in receiving compensation is a factor in generosity. «ما انفقتم... فهو یخلفه»

5- One of the reasons for the difference in provision is the testing of individuals. «یبسط، یقدر، ما انفقتم»

6- Expenditures are compensated. «فهو یخلفه» And Allah guarantees the compensation. «فهو یخلفه»

7- Expenditures are compensated in the best possible way. «خیر الرّازقین»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يَقُولُ لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ أَهَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِيَّاكُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْبُدُونَ (٤٠)
On the day He will muster them all together, He will say to the angels, ‘Was it you that these used to worship?’ (40)

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, the worshippers and the imaginary objects of worship will be gathered together. «یحشرهم جمیعاً»

2- In a period of history, angels were worshipped, and perhaps some idols were representations of angels. «للملائكة... ایّاكم... یعبدون»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, besides rebuking the polytheists, Allah will ask the objects of worship: Did you call the people to polytheism? This is done so that it is proven that the objects of worship themselves also condemn this action. «یقول للملائكة أهؤلاء ایّاكم كانوا یعبدون» Just as He says to Jesus عليه السلام: «أأنت قلت للنّاس اتّخذونى و أُمّى الهین»(519) Did you tell the people to take me and my mother as gods?

519) Ma'idah, 116.

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ أَنتَ وَلِيُّنَا مِن دُونِهِم ۖ بَلْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْبُدُونَ ٱلْجِنَّ ۖ أَكْثَرُهُم بِهِم مُّؤْمِنُونَ (٤۱)
They will say, ‘Immaculate are You! You are our wali, not they! No, they used to worship the jinn; most of them had faith in them.’ (41)

Key Points

Some polytheists worshipped the angels hoping to receive good things, but most of them worshipped the jinn for safety from evils. (520) Perhaps the intent behind the jinn in this Ayah is Satan, meaning that instead of worshipping the angels, they followed Satan.

520) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Let us learn the etiquette of speaking with Allah from the angels. «سبحانك انت ولیّنا»

2- Loyalty (Tawalli) towards Allah and disavowal (Tabarri) from others besides Him are found alongside each other. «انت ولیّنا من دونهم»

Ayah

فَٱلْيَوْمَ لَا يَمْلِكُ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ نَّفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا وَنَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ٱلَّتِى كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ (٤۲)
‘Today you have no power to benefit or harm one another,’ and We shall say to those who did wrong, ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.’ (42)

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah), sovereignty and ownership belong exclusively to Allah. (None of the worshipped entities—Jinn, angels, or others—possess any power; neither can the worshipped entity do anything for the worshipper, nor the worshipper for the worshipped entity). «فالیوم لا یملك بعضكم لبعض»

2- Turning towards other than Allah is oppression against oneself. «نقول للذین ظلموا»

3- That which results in the great wrath of Allah is continuous and malicious denial. «كنتم بها تكذبون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ قَالُوا۟ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا رَجُلٌ يُرِيدُ أَن يَصُدَّكُمْ عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُكُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ إِفْكٌ مُّفْتَرًى ۚ وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٤۳)
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, they say, ‘This is just a man who desires to keep you from what your fathers used to worship.’ And they say, ‘This is nothing but a fabricated lie.’ The faithless say of the truth when it comes to them: ‘This is nothing but plain magic,’ (43)

Key Points

The word Bayyināt (بیّنات), the plural of Bayyinah (بیّنة), is used for an evidence whose truth is manifest. And the word Ifk (اِفك) is used for something that has been inverted and turned away from its original form.

Messages

1- The divine verses are clear, and the denial of the disbelievers is due to their obstinacy. «بیّنات»

2- The method of denial used by the disbelievers consists of:

A: Belittling the leader. «ما هذا الا رجل»

B: Slander/Accusation. «یرید ان یصدّكم»

C: Relying on the path of their fathers and predecessors. «یعبد آباؤكم»

3- By presenting the ideological and practical path of their misguided ancestors, the disbelievers incited others to stand against the prophets. «یرید ان یصدكم عما كان یعبد آباؤكم»

4- Blind imitation is an obstacle to human progress. «یعبد آباؤكم»

5- The disbelievers oppose both the message and the messenger. «ما هذا الاّ رجل - ما هذا الاّ افك»

6- For their success, the disbelievers utilize every type of negative propaganda and emphasis. «ما هذا الاّ رجل - ما هذا الاّ افك - ان هذا الاّ سحر»

7- One of the obstacles to recognition (or knowledge) is disbelief (kufr). «قال الّذین كفروا للحقّ... ان هذا الاّ سحر»

Ayah

وَمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم مِّن كُتُبٍ يَدْرُسُونَهَا ۖ وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْهِمْ قَبْلَكَ مِن نَّذِيرٍ (٤٤)
though We did not give them any scriptures that they might have studied, nor did We send them any warner before you. (44)

Key Points

The concept of the Ayah is that: We did not give the polytheists of Mecca a book or a prophet before you, based on which they could determine whether you are right or wrong. Their denial is based solely on ignorance, obstinacy, and following the desires of themselves and their ancestors.

Messages

1- The affirmation or negation of any matter must be based on knowledge and revelation, and taking a stance without it is nothing more than desire (whim). «و ما آتیناهم...»

2- Heavenly books are for teaching and instruction. «كتب یدرسونها»

Ayah

وَكَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَمَا بَلَغُوا۟ مِعْشَارَ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوا۟ رُسُلِى ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ (٤٥)
Those who were before them had also denied—and these have not attained one-tenth of what We had given them—they impugned My apostles, so how was My rebuttal! (45)

Key Points

A similar verse is found in Ayah 21 of Surah Ghafir, where He says: «أولم یسیروا فى الارض فینظروا كیف كان عاقبة الّذین كانوا من قبلهم كانوا هم اشدّ منهم قوة و اثاراً» Did they not travel and journey through the land to see what was the end of those predecessors whose power and monuments were greater than theirs? We also read in Ayah 9 of Surah Ar-Rum: «... كانوا اشدّ منهم قوّة و اثاروا ألارض و عمروها اكثر ممّا عمروها» The power of the predecessors to cultivate the land was greater, but they were destroyed because they denied the Divine Prophets. Yes, civilization, powers, wealth, and transformations are not always progressing; rather, sometimes the resources and power of the past generations were greater.

Messages

1- Awareness of history makes great difficulties seem small and eases their endurance. «كذّب الّذین من قبلهم... كذّبوا رسلى»

2- Sometimes the enemy must be belittled. «و ما بلغوا معشار...»

3- The disbelievers denied the truth by relying on their power and wealth, and Allah said: Your wealth is only one-tenth compared to others. «و ما بلغوا معشار...»

4- Great populations and civilizations were destroyed because they denied the Prophets. (The polytheists of Mecca are nothing; do not worry.) «كذّبوا رسلى فكیف كان نكیر»

5- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is the same for all nations. «فكیف كان نكیر»

6- In education and guidance, sometimes the leverage of threat (especially an ambiguous threat) must be used. «فكیف كان نكیر»

Ayah

۞ قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَعِظُكُم بِوَٰحِدَةٍ ۖ أَن تَقُومُوا۟ لِلَّهِ مَثْنَىٰ وَفُرَٰدَىٰ ثُمَّ تَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ ۚ مَا بِصَاحِبِكُم مِّن جِنَّةٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ لَّكُم بَيْنَ يَدَىْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ (٤٦)
Say, ‘I give you just a single advice: that you rise up for Allah’s sake, in pairs or singly, and then reflect: there is no madness in your companion; he is just a warner to you before [the befalling of] a severe punishment.’ (46)

Key Points

1- One of the duties of the prophets is to admonish the people. «أَعِظُكُمْ»

2- Do not suffice merely with words; one must rise up. «تَقُومُوا»

3- What is important is a sincere uprising for the sake of Allah; numbers and personnel do not play the primary role. «تَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ مَثْنى‏ وَ فُرادى‏»

4- The uprising requires three elements:

Sincerity, «للّه»; a leader, «صاحبكم»; thought and planning. «تتفكّروا»

Movements and revolutions that are not for the sake of Allah, but arise from complexes, desires, or the pursuit of wealth, will not reach their goal. If a revolution is not accompanied by comprehensive thought and planning, or if the divine leader is not accompanying and associating with the revolutionaries, it will not achieve results and will lack permanence and blessings.

5- The Prophet does not have the slightest spiritual weakness. He has lived among you for years, and you all know him for his intellect and wisdom; attributing madness to him stems from thoughtlessness. «تتفكّروا ما بصاحبكم من جنّه»

6- The divine leader is a companion and speaker among the people. «صاحبكم»

7- Allah is the Protector of the prophets and repels accusations from them. «ما بصاحبكم من جنّة»

8- Although the work of the prophets is to give glad tidings and warning, warning receives more attention in the Quran, because heedless individuals have a greater need for warning. «ان هو الاّ نذیر»

9- The warning of the prophets is for the benefit of the people. «نذیر لكم»

10- The punishment is before you; if you do not rise up for the sake of Allah, you will be afflicted both in this world, and the Day of Judgment is not far off. «بین یدى عذاب»

Ayah

قُلْ مَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ فَهُوَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (٤۷)
Say, ‘Whatever reward I may have asked you is for your own good. My [true] reward lies only with Allah, and He is witness to all things.’ (47)

Key Points

It has been mentioned repeatedly in the Qur'an that the prophets did not ask the people for wages or rewards. In Surah Ash-Shu'ara, from Ayah 100 to 180, this point is reiterated through the words of various prophets. However, the Prophet of Islam requested a reward or wage in two instances: Once He said: «قل لا اسئلكم علیه اجراً الاّ المودّة فى القربى» (Say: I ask of you no reward for it but love for the near of kin) (521). I ask no wage from you except the affection for my Ahl al-Bayt (Household). And another time, in Ayah 57 of Surah Al-Furqan, He said: «قل لا اسالكم علیه من اجر الاّ مَن شاء أن یتّخذ الى ربّه سبیلا» (Say: I ask of you no reward for it, save that whoever wills may take a path to his Lord). I ask no wage from you for my mission, except that whoever wills may choose a path toward Allah. The Ayah under discussion combines the above verses, where the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله wishes to declare: If I request a wage from you and say, 'Love my Ahl al-Bayt,' it is because the benefit of this wage returns to you yourselves. «فهو لكم» (it is for you) because whoever is devoted to the infallible Ahl al-Bayt of the Noble Prophet علیهم السلام follows them, and whoever follows the infallible leaders has followed the path of Allah. «الاّ من شاء أن یتّخذ الى ربّه سبیلا» (save that whoever wills may take a path to his Lord). Thus, the benefit of this wage returns to us ourselves, like a teacher who tells his student, "I ask no wage from you except that you study my lesson well," the benefit of which returns to the student himself.

(521) Ash-Shura, 23.

Messages

1- Divine preachers must declare their lack of expectation (of worldly gain) to the people. (In the three verses related to the wage of the mission, the Prophet is commanded to express this using the word «قل» (Say): «قل... اِن اَجرى الاّ على اللّه»).

2- What is objectionable is asking for and requesting a wage, but if the people themselves give a gift to the religious preacher out of love and without coercion, that is a different matter. He says: «ما سالتكم» (I have not asked you), and He does not say: I will not accept.

3- The condition for the effectiveness of speech is that people recognize the leader as sincere and compassionate. «اِن اَجرى الاّ على اللّه» (If the leader has material expectations from the people, society will not tolerate him: «ام تسئلهم اجراً فهم من مغرم مثقلون» (522) Do you ask them for a wage, so that they are burdened by heavy debt?)

4- Choosing the path of Allah and the affection for the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, which is the wage of the mission, is in reality for the benefit of the people themselves, not the Noble Prophet. «فهو لكم»

5- Whoever knows that Allah is the Observer and Witness of his deeds places no hope in anyone other than Him. «اِن أجرى الاّ على اللّه و هو على كلّ شى ء شهید»

6- To dispel suspicions, we should take Allah as a witness to our sincerity. «اِن أجرى الاّ على اللّه و هو على كلّ شى ء شهید»

(522) Al-Qalam, 46.

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّى يَقْذِفُ بِٱلْحَقِّ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (٤۸)
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord hurls the truth. [He is] the knower of all that is Unseen.’ (48)

Messages

1- The realization of truth and the annihilation of falsehood are among the affairs of Allah's Lordship. «انّ ربّى یقذف بالحقّ»

2- Truth must attack falsehood and destroy it. («Yaqdhif» means to cast/throw and to assault/attack.)

3- The victory of truth over falsehood is among the Divine traditions. «انّ ربّى یقذف بالحقّ»

4- Establishing the truth and nullifying falsehood requires extensive information. «علاّم الغیوب»

Ayahs

قُلْ جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَمَا يُبْدِئُ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ (٤۹)
Say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood neither originates nor restores [anything].’ (49)
قُلْ إِن ضَلَلْتُ فَإِنَّمَآ أَضِلُّ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِى ۖ وَإِنِ ٱهْتَدَيْتُ فَبِمَا يُوحِىٓ إِلَىَّ رَبِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِيعٌ قَرِيبٌ (٥٠)
Say, ‘If I go astray, my going astray is only to my own harm, and if I am rightly guided that is because of what my Lord has revealed to me. Indeed He is all-hearing and nearmost.’ (50)

Key Points

Ibn Mas'ud, who was one of the companions of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, says: There were 360 idols in Mecca, which the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله threw down one after another on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca, reciting this noble Ayah: «قل جاء الحقّ...»(523)

Question: If the Qur'an and the Prophet of Truth have arrived, why does falsehood still persist?

Answer: Logical and intellectual sovereignty is different from practical and executive sovereignty. What pertains to Allah is the sending of the Truth (Haqq), and what pertains to us is the action and implementation of the Truth, which we have failed to do. Indeed, the patient's health depends on the correct prescription and the consumption of the medicine; one is the duty of the physician, and the other is the duty of the patient.

523) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Truth is victorious over falsehood. «جاء الحقّ و ما یبدى ء الباطل و ما یعید»

2- The Qur'an gives news of the unseen and the future. «و ما یبدى ء الباطل و ما یعید»

3- Without Divine revelation, even the Prophet is susceptible to deviation. «ان ضللت فانّما اضلّ على نفسى و ان اهتدیت فبما یوحى»

4- The dangers and ominous consequences of deviation return to the person himself. «اضلّ على نفسى»

5 - Allah both sends revelation and supervises its conveyance. «فبما یوحى الىّ ربّى انه سمیع قریب»

Ayahs

وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذْ فَزِعُوا۟ فَلَا فَوْتَ وَأُخِذُوا۟ مِن مَّكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ (٥۱)
Were you to see them when they will be stricken with terror, without any escape, and are seized from a close quarter. (51)
وَقَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ ٱلتَّنَاوُشُ مِن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٥۲)
They will say, ‘We believe in it [now]!’ But how can they attain it from a far-off place, (52)

Key Points

In these verses until the end of the Surah, a portrait of the heartbreaking state of the polytheists' death is depicted. The word "tanāwush" (تناوش) is derived from "nawsh" (نوش), meaning to take something easily. Narrations have appeared in both Shi'a and Sunni exegeses stating that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله interpreted these verses as referring to the emergence of Sufyani during the uprising of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, whom Allah will destroy from the nearest place. The meaning of "maqān qarīb" (مكان قریب - near place) is this world (Dunya), and "maqān ba'īd" (مكان بعید - distant place) is the Hereafter (Akhirah).

Messages

  1. The anguish, panic, and pleas for help from the polytheists will not solve any of their problems. "فزعوا فلا فوت" (They will panic, but there will be no escape). The polytheists have no refuge.
  2. Every disbeliever believes the moment he senses danger, but what good is it? (Faith based on choice is valuable, not faith based on necessity.) "اخذوا من مكان قریب - قالوا آمنّا" (They were seized from a near place - They said, 'We believe').
  3. Wherever death comes, it immediately seizes the person. "مكان قریب" (Near place).
  4. The punishment of the polytheists begins right in this world. "مكان قریب" (Near place).
  5. Returning to the world and compensating for the past is a distant and impossible path. "مكان بعید" (Distant place).

Ayahs

وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ مِن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٥۳)
when they denied it in the past—shooting at something invisible from a far-off place— (53)
وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ مَا يَشْتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشْيَاعِهِم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ فِى شَكٍّ مُّرِيبٍۭ (٥٤)
and a barrier is set between them and what they desire, as was done aforetime with their likes, who had remained in grave doubt? (54)

Messages

1- Those who yesterday leveled accusations against the Prophet and the Quran, today, finding themselves in distress, embrace faith. But what benefit is there? «و قد كفروا - و یقذفون»

2- The disbelievers lacked a clear understanding of the Prophet and the Quran; their accusations were like shooting arrows in the dark and casting stones from a distant place. «یقذفون بالغیب من مكان بعید»
